review_questions: Does CP00-153 cover exactly the 150 planned Risk C evidence terminal and review closeout units in order?
Can any CP00-153 evidence row execute Hermes commands, emit receipts, materialize templates, or write audit/product state?
Can any CP00-153 review row execute architecture/security review, evaluate permission bypass or audit completeness, record human approval, or expose hidden internals?
Are H03/C03 packet descriptors and PASS/PASS_WITH_FINDINGS/BLOCK semantics reference-only and non-authoritative?
Does CP00-153 hand off cleanly from CP00-152 to CP00-154 without implementing LDIP or splitting HRX?

parsed_review.summary: Complete read-only C03 review over the full CP00-153 scope including the now-tracked packages/audit/src/review-closeout-continuation-catalog.js; the prior sandboxed Not-logged-in attempt and the incomplete-scope attempt that missed the catalog and raised a P2 scope gap are correctly excluded — with the catalog present there is no scope gap. Coverage verified: 150 rows across RP03.P08.M06.S18-RP03.P09.M07.S02, first/last units match the binding, all source_unit_ids unique and in plan order. Distributions reconciled by hand and match tests/validator: evidence_mode P08 terminal 73 (3+22+20+20+8) and P09 review opening 77 (4+4+8+20+11+20+8+2) summing to 150; deliverable_counts implementation 63, test 9, hermes_evidence 38, claude_review 21, security_audit 14, ui 5 (sum 150). Handoff is clean: CP00-152 upstream inherited (first_unit RP03.P08.M06.S18 == old next_subphase_id), CP00-154/RP03.P09.M07.S03 declared, H03/C03 gates bound. Schema bump v0.19->v0.20 is consistent across contract.current_pack, review_closeout_continuation_pack, public_exports, hidden_source_fields, no_write_attestation, and the validator's matching checks. No hidden writes: every public function returns Object.freeze descriptors, helpers are module-private (no export leakage / collision), and no fs/network/DB mutation exists. Boundaries preserved: descriptor-only, no audit/product write, no runtime review, no Hermes command, no Claude send, implements_ldip:false and splits_hrx_product:false throughout. The two findings are P3, non-blocking, and inherent to the accepted descriptor-only design; nothing blocks pack or goal closeout, and pre-review validation (node --check, JSON parse, targeted and full node --test 86 pass, npm test 1160 pass, build, validate, rp03:audit:validate, git diff --check) is consistent with the diff.

findings.title: No-write attestation tests verify declarative constants, not intercepted runtime I/O
findings.details: The CP00-153 case tests assert booleans such as writes_product_state, appends_audit_event, executes_hermes_command, executes_architecture_review, evaluates_permission_bypass and sends_claude_prompt are false, but these are hardcoded constants stamped by freezeNoWriteResult and CP153_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION rather than the result of intercepting filesystem, network, or DB calls. The tests therefore guard the descriptor contract, not actual behavior: a future function that genuinely wrote state would still pass as long as the descriptor kept declaring false. This is acceptable here because the source itself contains no write/network/DB operations (verifiable by inspection) and this is the established, intentional pattern across CP00-135..CP00-152, so the no-write guarantee holds structurally. Noted only for the named validation-theater focus area.
findings.recommendation: No change required for closeout. If runtime catalogs are ever introduced under this program, replace declarative no-write assertions with behavioral guards (e.g., fs/network spies) so the attestation cannot drift from actual behavior.

findings.title: Hermes evidence packet stamps status passed for every input command without execution
findings.details: createAuditComplianceCp153HermesEvidencePacket(commands) maps each input command to { command, status: "passed" } unconditionally, and evidence_refs lists files (including docs/closeout-packs/cp00-153/manifest.json) that need not exist. This is consistent with descriptor-only intent — the returned packet self-declares executes_hermes_command:false and emits_hermes_evidence:false via freezeNoWriteResult, so it is a reference template rather than real gate evidence — and matches the CP00-152 packet pattern. The literal passed status could mislead if a downstream consumer ever treated this descriptor as authoritative H03 runtime evidence.
findings.recommendation: No change required for closeout. Consider a neutral status token (e.g., descriptor_only) for clarity to make explicit that the packet records no execution result.
